rules.rule: User-visible strings in packages/ui/src/native/** must use useSdkTranslation() with t('key') or <Trans i18nKey="key">. Add every new key under reactnative.* in platform-localization (sources/common/en.json); synced locale JSON arrives via the Distribute React Native Localization workflow. Flag any newly added or changed user-visible English literal in Text children, accessibilityLabel, accessibilityHint, placeholder, alert/Alert strings, and SDK-owned headerTitle values — not just known examples like Cancel, Loading, Books, or Versions. Do not flag packages/ui/src/dom/** (ADR 0009), __tests__/**, *.test.*, consumer-provided prop pass-through, or non-user-facing literals (test IDs, log messages, style tokens, route names). See docs/contributing/native-i18n.md.

Translation JSON under packages/ui/src/i18n/locales/ (en.json, es.json, fr.json, and any future locale) is generated and synced from the platform-localization repo. Do not hand-edit these files or add/change/remove string values directly in a PR. New keys must be added under reactnative.* in platform-localization (sources/common/en.json) and arrive via the 'Distribute React Native Localization' workflow. Flag any diff that adds or edits string values in these files. Exceptions: the automated localization sync PR, and locales/index.ts (locale registration, which is hand-editable).
